title: Delta and Joby Aviation Partner to Pioneer Home-to-Airport Transportation to Customers
text: Delta Air Lines has partnered with Joby Aviation to pioneer electric air transportation from homes to airports. Delta Air Lines is making an upfront equity investment of $60 million in Joby, with potential expansion to $200 million upon achieving operational milestones. The initial service will operate in New York and Los Angeles, targeting customers booking Delta flights. Joby operates all-electric vertical takeoff and landing aircraft designed for fast, quiet city-to-airport journeys. The aircraft has completed over 1,000 test flights and received FAA Part 135 Air Carrier Certification. The arrangement will be mutually exclusive across the U.S. and U.K. for five years following commercial launch, with potential extension. Delta customers will access booking through Delta's platforms alongside Joby's standard airport service. Delta CEO Ed Bastian stated this represents a groundbreaking opportunity for Delta to deliver a time-saving, uniquely premium home-to-airport solution. The partnership integrates seamless booking and zero-emission transportation, complementing Delta's existing investments in airport modernization and digital identity technology.
